carbon_policy_signal.item: EU ETS industry pressure to freeze carbon pricing; political momentum for weakening cost pass-through rules and free-allocation guardrails under review.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Weakening political support for carbon cost pass-through lowers thermal generation marginal cost and cools merit-order incentive for coal-exit; may suppress EUA carry and flatten 2026+ power curve.

risk_flags: EU storage at 44.7% (20th pctile, 14.3 pp below seasonal)—refill velocity into summer will set H2 thermal call and winter margin adequacy.

Clean Spark at 91st percentile (34.54 EUR/MWh) despite TTF at median (42.51 EUR/MWh); implies gas-coal merit-order shift or carbon-driven premium—EUA at 40th pctile (33.4 EUR/tCO2) does not explain spark strength alone.
Iran-US Hormuz deal removes oil supply disruption risk (bearish crude/LNG arb into EU); TTF gains 2.4% weekly cushion but upside capped if arb unwinds.
ETS industry freeze demand signals weakening carbon cost pass-through political support; EUA outsized 2.77% daily move (2.1σ) flags volatility risk into policy clarity window.

base.summary: Renewables moderate to 60–65th percentile; storage refill steady; clean spark normalizes to 50th pctile as thermal call flattens.

downside.summary: Renewables sustained >85th pctile; mild weather extends; LNG arb weakens post-Hormuz as Brent softens; thermal margin squeezes further.

upside.summary: Cold front or renewable forecast miss; storage refill lags seasonal by >20 pp; TTF strength and thermal dispatch surge ahead of H2.

themes: Renewables suppressing dispatch; thermal spreads paradoxically elevated
Storage deficit risks H2 refill pace and winter margin
Hormuz reopening eases crude-LNG arb but ETS industry pressure clouds carbon outlook
top_drivers: Renewables at 93rd percentile (70.98% of load) compressing thermal dispatch window and merit order; clean spark remains elevated despite gas at median, signaling coal-to-gas fuel switch is in-the-money.
EU storage 14.3 pp below seasonal (44.7%, 20th pctile) tightens H2 refill expectation; summer injection pace will set winter margin risk and H2 power curve steepness.
Iran-US Hormuz deal removes crude supply disruption tail; unwinds LNG arb premium into EU, easing TTF-Brent correlation and power fuel-switch economics in base case.
top_takeaway: Renewables at 93rd percentile suppress thermal; clean spark near 11-year highs despite gas at 52nd percentile—storage 14 pp below seasonal signals tight H2 refill risk.

watchlist.why: 14.3 pp below seasonal average; summer injection rate will set H2 power risk and winter margin. Monitor weekly storage reports through August.
Paradoxically elevated vs. TTF median; coal-to-gas spread (clean dark at 75th pctile) or carbon pass-through driving signal. Watch if renewables drop or storage pressures merit order.

watchlist.metric: EUA volatility (2.77% daily move, 2.1σ; 40th pctile level)
watchlist.why: Industry pressure to freeze ETS pricing and pending EU policy clarity create tail risk. Carbon curve steepness and free-allocation signals matter for 2026+ thermal dispatch economics.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz strait & LNG arb (TTF vs. JKM)
watchlist.why: Iran-US deal removes closure risk premium; watch charter rates and arb spreads for crude-driven upside/downside transmission into EU gas cost base over next 2–4 weeks.

watchlist.metric: GB Power premium (118.19 EUR/MWh, 83rd pctile) vs. DE (131.85 EUR/MWh, 74th pctile)
watchlist.why: GB-DE spread has room; interconnector flows and storage imbalance between regions may affect spread carry and renewable dispatch sequencing.

watchlist_dated.name: G7 Evian summit (energy/Iran outcomes)
watchlist_dated.why: Hormuz and energy sanctions clarity feeds into crude cost and LNG arb stability; feeds TTF curve expectation for H2 supply.
